sanskrit_iast: īśā vāsyamidaṁ sarvaṁ yatkiñca jagatyāṁ jagat | tena tyaktena bhuñjīthā mā gṛdhaḥ kasyasviddhanam ||
meaning: ईशा वास्यमिदं सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत्‌। तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्यस्विद्धनम्‌ ॥
text: All this is for habitation by the Lord, whatsoever is individual universe of movement in the universal motion. By that renounced thou shouldst enjoy; lust not after any man's possession.

sanskrit_iast: kurvanneveha karmāṇi jijīviṣet śataṁ samāḥ | evaṁ tvayi nānyatheto'sti na karma lipyate nare ||
meaning: कुर्वन्नेवेह कर्माणि जिजीविषेत् शतं समाः। एवं त्वयि नान्यथेतोऽस्ति न कर्म लिप्यते नरे ॥
text: Doing verily works in this world one should wish to live a hundred years. Thus it is in thee and not otherwise than this; action cleaves not to a man.

sanskrit_iast: asuryā nāma te lokā andhena tamasāvṛtāḥ | tāṁste pretyābhigacchanti ye ke cātmahano janāḥ ||
meaning: असूर्या नाम ते लोका अन्धेन तमसावृताः।तांस्ते प्रेत्याभिगच्छन्ति ये के चात्महनो जनाः ॥
text: Sunless are those worlds and enveloped in blind gloom whereto all they in their passing hence resort who are slayers of their souls.

sanskrit_iast: anejadekaṁ manaso javīyo nainaddevā āpnuvan pūrvamarṣat | taddhāvato'nyānatyeti tiṣṭhat tasminnapo mātariśvā dadhāti ||
meaning: अनेजदेकं मनसो जवीयो नैनद्देवा आप्नुवन् पूर्वमर्षत्‌। तद्धावतोऽन्यानत्येति तिष्ठत् तस्मिन्नपो मातरिश्वा दधाति ॥
text: One unmoving that is swifter than Mind, That the Gods reach not, for It progresses ever in front. That, standing, passes beyond others as they run. In That the Master of Life establishes the Waters.

sanskrit_iast: tadejati tannaijati tad dūre tadvantike | tadantarasya sarvasya tadu sarvasyāsya bāhyataḥ ||
meaning: तदेजति तन्नैजति तद् दूरे तद्वन्तिके। तदन्तरस्य सर्वस्य तदु सर्वस्यास्य बाह्यतः ॥
text: That moves and That moves not; That is far and the same is near; That is within all this and That also is outside all this.

sanskrit_iast: yastu sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmanyevānupaśyati | sarvabhūteṣu cātmānaṁ tato na vijugupsate ||
meaning: यस्तु सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मन्येवानुपश्यति। सर्वभूतेषु चात्मानं ततो न विजुगुप्सते ॥
text: But he who sees everywhere the Self in all existences and all existences in the Self, shrinks not thereafter from aught.

sanskrit_iast: yasmin sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmaivābhūd vijānataḥ | tatra ko mohaḥ kaḥ śoka ekatvamanupaśyataḥ ||
meaning: यस्मिन् सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मैवाभूद् विजानतः। तत्र को मोहः कः शोक एकत्वमनुपश्यतः ॥
text: He in whom it is the Self - Being that has become all existences that are Becomings, for he has the perfect knowledge, how shall he be deluded, whence shall he have grief who sees everywhere oneness ?

sanskrit_iast: sa paryagācchukramakāyamavraṇamasnāviraṁ śuddhamapāpaviddham | kavirmanīṣī paribhūḥ svayambhūryāthātathyato'rthān vyadadhāt śāśvatībhyaḥ samābhyaḥ ||
meaning: स पर्यगाच्छुक्रमकायमव्रणमस्नाविरं शुद्धमपापविद्धम्‌। कविर्मनीषी परिभूः स्वयम्भूर्याथातथ्यतोऽर्थान्‌ व्यदधात् शाश्वतीभ्यः समाभ्यः ॥
text: It is He that has gone abroad-That which is bright, bodiless, without scar of imperfection, without sinews, pure, unpierced by evil. The Seer, the Thinker, the One who becomes everywhere, the Self - existent has ordered objects perfectly according to their nature from years sempiternal.

sanskrit_iast: andhaṁ tamaḥ praviśanti ye'vidyāmupāsate | tato bhūya iva te tamo ya u vidyāyāṁ ratāḥ ||
meaning: अन्धं तमः प्रविशन्ति येऽविद्यामुपासते। ततो भूय इव ते तमो य उ विद्यायां रताः ॥
text: Into a blind darkness they enter who follow after the Ignorance, they as if into a greater darkness who devote themselves to the Knowledge alone.

sanskrit_iast: anyadevāhurvidyayā'nyadāhuravidyayā | iti śuśruma dhīrāṇāṁ ye nastadvicacakṣire ||
meaning: अन्यदेवाहुर्विद्ययाऽन्यदाहुरविद्यया। इति शुश्रुम धीराणां ये नस्तद्विचचक्षिरे ॥
text: Other, verily, it is said, is that which comes by the Knowledge, other that which comes by the Ignorance; this is the lore we have received from the wise who revealed That to our understanding.

sanskrit_iast: vidyāñcāvidyāñca yastadvedobhayaṁ saha | avidyayā mṛtyuṁ tīrtvā vidyayā'mṛtamaśnute ||
meaning: विद्याञ्चाविद्याञ्च यस्तद्वेदोभयं सह। अविद्यया मृत्युं तीर्त्वा विद्ययाऽमृतमश्नुते ॥
text: He who knows That as both in one, the Knowledge and the Ignorance, by the Ignorance crosses beyond death and by the Knowledge enjoys Immortality.

sanskrit_iast: andhaṁ tamaḥ praviśanti ye'sambhūtimupāsate | tato bhūya iva te tamo ya u sambhūtyāṁ ratāḥ ||
meaning: अन्धं तमः प्रविशन्ति येऽसम्भूतिमुपासते। ततो भूय इव ते तमो य उ सम्भूत्यां रताः ॥
text: Into a blind darkness they enter who follow after the Non - Birth, they as if into a greater darkness who devote themselves to the Birth alone.

sanskrit_iast: anyadevāhuḥ sambhavādanyadāhurasambhavāt | iti śuśruma dhīrāṇāṁ ye nastadvicacakṣire ||
meaning: अन्यदेवाहुः सम्भवादन्यदाहुरसम्भवात्‌। इति शुश्रुम धीराणां ये नस्तद्विचचक्षिरे ॥
text: Other, verily, it is said, is that which comes by the Birth, other that which comes by the Non - Birth; this is the lore we have received from the wise who revealed That to our understanding.

sanskrit_iast: sambhūtiñca vināśañca yastadvedobhayaṁ saha | vināśena mṛtyuṁ tīrtvā sambhūtyā'mṛtamaśnute ||
meaning: सम्भूतिञ्च विनाशञ्च यस्तद्वेदोभयं सह। विनाशेन मृत्युं तीर्त्वा सम्भूत्याऽमृतमश्नुते ॥
text: He who knows That as both in one, the Birth and the dissolution of Birth, by the dissolution crosses beyond death and by the Birth enjoys Immortality.

sanskrit_iast: hiraṇmayena pātreṇa satyasyāpihitaṁ mukham | tat tvaṁ pūṣannapāvṛṇu satyadharmāya dṛṣṭaye ||
meaning: हिरण्मयेन पात्रेण सत्यस्यापिहितं मुखम्‌। तत् त्वं पूषन्नपावृणु सत्यधर्माय दृष्टये ॥
text: The face of Truth is covered with a brilliant golden lid; that do thou remove, O Fosterer, for the law of the Truth, for sight.

sanskrit_iast: pūṣannekarṣe yama sūrya prājāpatya vyūha raśmīn samūha | tejo yat te rūpaṁ kalyāṇatamaṁ tatte paśyāmi yo'sāvasau puruṣaḥ so'hamasmi ||
meaning: पूषन्नेकर्षे यम सूर्य प्राजापत्य व्यूह रश्मीन्‌ समूह। तेजो यत् ते रूपं कल्याणतमं तत्ते पश्यामि योऽसावसौ पुरुषः सोऽहमस्मि ॥
text: 16. O Fosterer, O sole Seer, O Ordainer, O illumining Sun, O power of the Father of creatures, marshal thy rays, draw together thy light; the Lustre which is thy most blessed form of all, that in Thee I behold. The Purusha there and there, He am I.

sanskrit_iast: vāyuranilamamṛtamathedaṁ bhasmāṁtaṁ śarīram | om krato smara kṛtaṁ smara krato smara kṛtaṁ smara ||
meaning: वायुरनिलममृतमथेदं भस्मांतं शरीरम्‌। ॐ क्रतो स्मर कृतं स्मर क्रतो स्मर कृतं स्मर ॥
text: The Breath of things is an immortal Life, but of this body ashes are the end. OM! OWill, remember, that which was done remember! O Will, remember, that which was done remember.

sanskrit_iast: agne naya supathā rāye asmān viśvāni deva vayunāni vidvān | yuyodhyasmajjuhurāṇameno bhūyiṣṭhāṁ te namauktiṁ vidhema ||
meaning: अग्ने नय सुपथा राये अस्मान्‌ विश्वानि देव वयुनानि विद्वान्‌। युयोध्यस्मज्जुहुराणमेनो भूयिष्ठां ते नम‍उक्तिं विधेम ॥
text: O god Agni, knowing all things that are manifested, lead us by the good path to the felicity; remove from us the devious attraction of sin. To thee completest speech of submission we would dispose.
